post.summary:
[MOD-CO-MSG-1] Create New Corporation — atomically create an x/group
group + group policy and register the resulting policy_address as a new
Corporation VPR entry.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
MsgCreateCorporation — MOD-CO-MSG-1.

Signer is the submitting account; no privilege required. Forwarded as the
`admin` field of the underlying `MsgCreateGroupWithPolicy`. After execution,
the signer holds no ongoing admin privileges over the group, group policy,
or Corporation — the group_policy_address becomes the admin of both the
group and the group policy (`group_policy_as_admin` is hardcoded to `true`).

post.summary:
[MOD-CO-MSG-2] Update Corporation — rotate the DID of an existing
Corporation. Signers: corporation (policy_address) + operator.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
MsgUpdateCorporation — MOD-CO-MSG-2. Rotates the DID of an existing
Corporation. `language` is immutable and cannot be updated.

post.summary:
UpdateParams defines a (governance) operation for updating the module
parameters. The authority defaults to the x/gov module account.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: MsgUpdateParams is the Msg/UpdateParams request type.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

address.description: address is the bech32 address of the member.

weight.description:
weight is the member's voting weight. MUST be a non-zero decimal string
representing a positive number (e.g. "1", "10").

metadata.description: metadata is optional arbitrary metadata attached to the member.

verana.co.v1.Member.description:
Member is the MOD-CO-local mirror of cosmos.group.v1.MemberRequest. We don't
import the x/group proto directly because the buf cosmos-sdk dep pinned by
this repo predates the x/group proto export. The Go keeper converts this
type to the cosmos.group.v1.MemberRequest before calling x/group.

members.description: members is the initial group membership list. MUST contain at least one.

decision_policy.description:
decision_policy must encode a cosmos.group.v1.ThresholdDecisionPolicy or
cosmos.group.v1.PercentageDecisionPolicy. Passed through to x/group.

did.description: did is the DID of the new Corporation. MUST be globally unique.

language.description: language is the primary language tag (BCP 47).

doc_url.description: doc_url is the URL of the v1 CGF document.

doc_digest_sri.description: doc_digest_sri is the digest_sri of the v1 CGF document.

verana.co.v1.MsgCreateCorporation.description:
MsgCreateCorporation — MOD-CO-MSG-1.

Signer is the submitting account; no privilege required. Forwarded as the
`admin` field of the underlying `MsgCreateGroupWithPolicy`. After execution,
the signer holds no ongoing admin privileges over the group, group policy,
or Corporation — the group_policy_address becomes the admin of both the

verana.co.v1.MsgUpdateCorporation.description:
MsgUpdateCorporation — MOD-CO-MSG-2. Rotates the DID of an existing
Corporation. `language` is immutable and cannot be updated.

verana.co.v1.MsgUpdateParams.description: MsgUpdateParams is the Msg/UpdateParams request type.

verana.co.v1.Params.description:
Params defines the parameters for the x/co module.
The module has no parameters at this time; the message is kept for forward
compatibility so that gov-proposed parameter changes can land without proto
migrations.
